How Do You Write a Ternary Operator in CoffeeScript? (Spoiler: You Don't)
Just jotting down a quick note on how to write a ternary operator in CoffeeScript, since it's not what you'd expect.
Normally in JavaScript you'd write a ternary like this:
language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

var a = true;
a ? true : false
But CoffeeScript doesn't support that syntax. Instead you have to write it like this:
language-coffee shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

a = true;
if a then true else false
if a then true else false
Writing the ternary operator correctly in CoffeeScript
If you go ahead and use the regular JS ternary syntax, you'll just get an error:
Trying to write a ternary the JS way in CoffeeScript
There are a couple of other ways to write this too, so I'll note those down as well:

a = true

console.log "Hello #{ if a then "Jack" else "Mark"}"

CoffeeScript doesn't support the usual JavaScript ternary operator syntax, so if you try the classic `a ? b : c` you'll just get an error. Here's a quick note on how to write the equivalent in CoffeeScript using if/then/else, including a couple of variations inside string interpolation.
